claim: Verification confirms that gathered evidence properly separates facts from assumptions, all evidence paths are usable and accurate, and unresolved missing data is correctly reflected in blockers and assumptions arrays. The explorer and cross-checker results show no assumptions promoted to facts, and evidence agreement is achieved with no conflicts.

evidence.summary: Provider-specific executionConfig implementation verified by both explorer and cross-checker with matching line numbers and summaries
evidence.why: Evidence agreement between independent agents confirms this is factual project implementation, not assumption.

evidence.summary: Cross-provider isolation, tool profile, and boundary enforcement tests independently verified by cross-checker with accurate line ranges
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms implementation behavior described in code evidence.

evidence.summary: Doctrine documentation verified by both agents as specifying evidence agent protocols and cross-provider leakage prevention requirements
evidence.why: Documentation evidence confirms product requirements match implementation.

evidence.summary: Previous evidence results show all evidence items have proper scope=project, repo-relative paths, and line numbers; no evidence items have scope=unknown or user
evidence.why: Verification that evidence protocol compliance is maintained across all agent results.

evidence.summary: Explorer has empty assumptions array; cross-checker has 3 properly scoped assumptions (Codex defaults, Claude boundary sufficiency, custom provider contract)
evidence.why: Evidence that assumptions are properly separated from facts and not promoted to evidence claims.

evidence.summary: Missing data (stress testing, provider capability documentation, edge case test coverage) properly reflected in missing arrays, not as assumptions
evidence.why: Verification that unresolved gaps are correctly categorized as missing rather than promoted to facts.

evidence.summary: Evidence agreement shows pass status with zero conflicts, confirming no factual contradictions between explorer and cross-checker
evidence.why: Independent cross-check achieved agreement without averaging away differences.

risks: The incomplete Codex model inheritance check at line 445-447 could allow edge cases where model normalization fails to clear inherited Claude models
Boundary enforcement reliability depends on git availability; non-git projects or sparse checkouts may have reduced detection accuracy
Tool profile normalization only applies to Claude provider; Codex and custom providers may have undocumented tool capability differences

result.notes: Evidence protocol compliance verified: all evidence properly scoped with usable paths, assumptions separated from facts, and missing data correctly reflected. Explorer and cross-checker achieved agreement without conflicts. Main remaining gap is runtime stress testing with real agents, which is properly documented as missing rather than assumed. The evidence is sufficient to support PRD readiness for careful route.
